title: scNMT-seq
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"8fkhd","text":"Here we describe the full wetlab protocol for scNMT-seq (single-cell nucleosome position, methylome and transcriptome sequencing), a plate-based single-cell multi-omic method.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cm6au","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4nadi","text":"In scNMT-seq, a GpC methyltransferase is used to label non-nucleosome bound DNA thus encoding chromatin accessiblity information within the genomic DNA. Bisulfite conversion is then used to discriminate the two chromatin states which can be read from GpC dinucleotide positions. Endogenous DNA methylation is read from the CpG positions of the same molecules and gene expression data is obtained by capturing poly-A RNA on magnetic beads and pysically separating before the gDNA is bisulfite converted. This mRNA on beads is pocessed using Smart-seq2. The protocol is carried out in 96w plates and typically takes 3-4 days to complete.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"54aae","text":"scNMT-seq is essentially a combination of the following methods:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2em6a","text":"1. NOMe-seq:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"b45jg","text":"        Genome-wide mapping of nucleosome positioning and DNA methylation within individual DNA molecules","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":8,"length":97}],"data":[]},{"key":"dv15v","text":"        Kelly T., Liu Y. et al 2012","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":25,"length":5}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4f29q","text":"        doi: 10.1101/gr.143008.112","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4uupo","text":"2. G\u0026T-seq:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5gscm","text":"        G\u0026T-seq: parallel sequencing of single-cell genomes and transcriptomes","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":8,"length":70}],"data":[]},{"key":"bn8tp","text":"        Macaulay I. et al 2015","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":20,"length":5}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"kare","text":"        doi: 10.1038/nmeth.3370","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"dekla","text":"3. Smartseq2:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"80bqt","text":"        Smart-seq2 for sensitive full-length transcriptome profiling in single cells","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":2,"offset":8,"length":76}],"data":[]},{"key":"7d3pf","text":"        Picelli S. et al 2013","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":19,"length":5}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4ph8a","text":"        doi: 10.1038/nmeth.2639","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ark5r","text":"4. scBS-seq:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bqvk4","text":"       Single-cell genome-wide bisulfite sequencing for assessing epigenetic heterogeneity","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":3,"offset":7,"length":83}],"data":[]},{"key":"504s5","text":"        Smallwood S., Lee H. et al 2014","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":29,"length":5}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"89e3p","text":"        doi: 10.1038/nmeth.3035","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5dlrp","text":"        ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"2pcp5","text":"Protocols for each of these individual methods have been published elsewhere, however the full step-by-step protocol for scNMT-seq had not previously been available as a single document.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"405u9","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"ckaft","text":"General guidelines","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":18}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"86rl0","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"a4ngo","text":"As with other single-cell sequencing methods it is important to minimise sources of contamination. We perform all pre-PCR pipetting steps in a dedicated pre-PCR room. Prior to starting, all surfaces and pipettes are wiped down with a dilute bleach solution (e.g. RNAse away or similar) to mitigate RNase and DNA contamination and all tubes and PCR plates are subjected to UV iradiation. We also reccomend aliquoting reagents such as oligos and dNTPs so that each tube is used once then discarded.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4u9u9","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"f80pr","text":"We use an automated pipetting robot (Agilent Bravo Workstation) for each of the pre-PCR magnetic bead portions of the protocol. This reduces hands-on time and increases reproducibility of the method. However, it is also possible to perform the whole protocol by hand with an appropriate magnet and multichanel pipettes. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cph04","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"c3bbn","text":"Sequencing of libraries should be possible on any Illumina instrument unless certain restrictions are programmed in such as with the X10. We routinely use HiSeq 2000/2500 v4 and NextSeq500. RNA-seq libraries should be sequenced on a separate lane or flowcell to the BS-seq libraries. We have had success with read-depths of 0.5-2M per cell for the RNA-seq and 2-10M per cell for the BS-seq. We reccommend paired-end with mid-long read lengths (75-125bp) for the BS-seq to maximise cytosine coverage. However, it is possible that much lower read-depths could yeild informative data, especially in studies with large numbers of cells.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"adbuo","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"7fv3u","text":"Indexes are incorporated into the BS-seq libraries during the final amplification step. We use the iPCRTag indexing system, which means that a custom index read primer needs to be spiked-in with the other i7 index primers for sequencing. However, another indexing system (e.g. Truseq) could be used by re-desigining the second strand synthesis oligo (SSO) appropriately. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3pepc","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"93CDD9806B3911E9B962794768B6BF9F","url":"https://doi.org/10.1101/gr.143008.112"}},"1":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"2F88E0206B3C11E9B962794768B6BF9F","url":"https://doi.org/10.1038/nmeth.3370"}},"2":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"DABC80606B3B11E9B962794768B6BF9F","url":"https://doi.org/10.1038/nmeth.2639"}},"3":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"0AA8DF806B3C11E9B962794768B6BF9F","url":"https://doi.org/10.1038/nmeth.3035"}}}}
steps: Collect single cells in 2.5 µL, of freshly prepared GpC methylase reaction mix (keep chilled on ice) Centrifuge at ≥1000g for ≥10s. We have successfully used both flow sorting and manual pipetting for single-cell collections.
Incubate on a thermocycler: 00:15:00 37 °C
Add 5 µL, RLT plus Centrifuge at ≥1000g for ≥10s.
Store at -80 °C
Pipette 55 µL, Dynabeads (MyOne Streptavidin C1) into a microcentrifuge tube. Place on a magnet and remove supernatent.
Resuspend beads in 55 µL, solution A (0.1M NaOH, 0.05M NaCl) by pippetting. Place on a magnet and remove supernatent.
Resuspend beads in 55 µL, solution B (0.1M NaCl) . Place on magnet and remove supernatent.
Resuspend beads in 55 µL, 2x B&W (2 M NaCl, 10 mM Tris-HCl, 1 mM EDTA) . Place on magnet and remove supernatent.

Resuspend beads in 55 µL, 2x B&W and 55 µL, bio-Smartseq2-dT (100uM) . bio-Smartseq2-dT: 5'-Biotin-TEG-AAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGTACTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTVN-3' (IDT, purified with HPLC and resuspended in nuclease free water)
Rotate tube containing beads such that beads remain in solution for a minimum of 00:15:00 . Beads can be prepared in advance and stored at 4 °C for up to 1 month
Place on magnet and remove supernatent. Resuspend beads in 110 µL, 1x B&W buffer (1M NaCl, 5mM Tris, 0.5mM EDTA) . Place on magnet and remove supernatent.

Place on magnet and remove supernatent. Resuspend beads in: 860 µL, Nuclease free water , 220 µL, 5x first strand buffer (Invitrogen, Superscript II) , 28 µL, RNasin (Promega) , 10 µL, Diluted ERCC spike-in This assumes 2.5ul GpC reaction plus 5ul RLTplus. If using bigger volumes, adjust upward in proportion. ERCC spike-in is optional. The concentration used will depend on cell type (RNA content).
Manually pipette 10 µL of prepared oligo-dT beads to each well of the sample plate using a multichannel pipette.
Prepare G&T-seq wash buffer: %s %s
Prepare RT mastermix: %s %s

Mix at maximum speed for 00:10:00 We perform mRNA separation steps on an Agilent Bravo liquid handling robot. If performing this step manually, intermittent vortexing for 10 minutes achieves the same result.
Place on magnet until beads pelette. Aspirate 17.5 µL and transfer to empty lobind plate for gDNA collection.
Add 15 µL of G&T-seq wash buffer off magnet.
Mix at maximum speed for 00:01:00 If performing manually, a thorough vortexing here suffices.
go to step #22 Lysate (17.5ul) combined with 3 washes (15ul each) should now have been collected into the gDNA plate Pause point. gDNA can be frozen at -20C for later processing.
Add 10 µL, RT mastermix to beads.
Mix at maximum speed for 00:01:00
Incubate on a thermocycler as follows: 01:00:00 42 °C 00:30:00 50 °C 00:10:00 60 °C Vortex plate at 30 and 60 minute time points to resuspend beads.
Add 12 µL PCR mastermix by hand using a multichannel pipette. %s %s

Cycle as follows: 00:03:00 98 °C 18 Cycles of: 00:00:20 98 °C 00:00:15 67 °C 00:06:00 72 °C Followed by: 00:05:00 72 °C 00:00:00, pause - usually overnight 4 °C Cycle number depends on cell type. 18 is sufficient for mouse ES cells and embryo cells. Pause point. cDNA can be frozen at -20C for later processing.
This section is performed outside of pre-PCR room. Add 18 µL AMPure XP beads. Mix thoroughly and incubate for 5 minutes.
Add 50 µL AMPure XP beads to gDNA samples. Vortex thoroughly and incubate for 00:30:00

Bisulfite reagent is prepared according to manufacturers instructions (Zymo EZ-methylation direct): Add 7.9 mL, M-Solubilization Buffer and 3 mL, M-Dilution Buffer to a bottle of CT Conversion Reagent. Mix at room temperature with frequent vortexing or shaking for at least 10 minutes. Add 1.6 mL, M-Reaction Buffer and vortex thoroughly.
Place gDNA sample plate on magnet to pellet beads.

Add 65 µL of prepared CT Conversion Reagent solution to each sample on the magnet. Do not mix sample and beads with CT reagent – beads should remain pelleted during conversion to avoid excessive disolving of magnetic beads.
Incubate on a thermocycler as follows: 98 °C 00:08:00 64 °C 03:00:00 4 °C, pause until purification (maximum duration of overnight)
Add 533 µL, Zymo Magbinding beads to 32 mL, M-Binding buffer , mix and dispense 305 µL per well of deepwell plate using multichannel and reservoir.

Add sample to deepwell plate containing beads and binding buffer. Mix thoroughly then transfer 100 µL back to sample plate, mix thoroughly and transfer back to deepwell plate to maximise recovery of bisuflite converted DNA. Mix for 00:05:00
Prepare first strand master mix for elution: %s %s First strand oligo: /5SpC3/CTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCTNNNNNN (IDT, HPLC purified). This primer contains the Illumina PE read 1 sequence.
Resuspend beads in 20 µL, first strand synthesis mix . incubate at 60 °C 00:05:00
Place on magnet until beads pellet. Transfer 20 µL supernatent to fresh lobind PCR plate.
Place sample plate on thermocycler at 65 °C 00:03:00 then immediately cool on ice.
Add 1 µL Klenow exo- (50 U/ul, Enzymatics) using multichannel and PCR strip.
Incubate on a thermocyler as follows: 4 °C 00:05:00 Slow ramp from 4 °C to 37 °C at 15s per 1C 37 °C 00:30:00 4 °C hold.
95 °C 00:00:45 95 °C for 45 seconds then immediately cool 4 °C using two ice blocks.
Add 2.5 µL, first-strand extra cycles mix %s %s First strand oligo: /5SpC3/CTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCTNNNNNN (IDT, HPLC purified). This primer contains the Illumina PE read 1 sequence.
go to step #69
50 µL, Exonuclease mix %s %s
Add 70 µL, AMPureXP beads per well of a deepwell plate. Transfer 100 µL sample to deepwell plate. Mix thoroughly and incubate at room temperature for 10 minutes.
Place plate on magent and wait until beads pellet. Remove supernatent.
Add 180 µL, 80% ethanol off the magnet and mix thoroughly with pipetting.
Place plate on magent and wait until beads pellet. Remove supernatent.

Resuspend beads in 49 µL, second strand master mix %s %s Second strand oligo: TGCTGAACCGCTCTTCCGATCTNNNNNN (HPLC purified from IDT). This primer contains the Illumina PE read 2 sequence. Users wishing to use Truseq indexing primers (instead of iPCRTag) should redesign this so that it matches the Truseq read 2 sequence (TCAGACGTGTGCTCTTCCGATC).
Transfer second strand mix containing beads to a fresh 96w PCR plate.
Incubate on a thermocyler: 98 °C 00:02:00 , then immediatly cool on ice.
Add 0.5 µL, Klenow exo- (50U/ul, Enzymatics) and incubate: 4 °C 00:05:00 Slow ramp from 4 °C to 37 °C at 15s per 1C 37 °C 01:30:00 4 °C hold.
Prepare an aliquot of AMPure buffer by pelleting AMPure XP beads using a magnet or centrifuge and taking the supernatent.
Add 70 µL, AMPure buffer and 50 µL, water per well of a deepwell plate.
Transfer sample, containing beads, to the deepwell plate containing AMPure buffer and water. Mix thoroughly then incubate at room temperature 00:10:00
Place plate on magnet and wait until beads pellet.
Remove supernatent.
Wash twice with 80% ethanol.
Resuspend beads in 48 µL, PCR master mix : %s %s PE1.0: AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAGATCTACACTCTTTCCCTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATC*T (IDT, HPLC) This primer contains the full Illumina P5 and PE read 1 sequences.

Add 2 µL, iTAG indexing primer (5uM) We prepare iTAG primers in 96w plates for this step. iPCRTag: CAAGCAGAAGACGGCATACGAGATXXXXXXXXGAGATCGGTCTCGGCATT CCTGCTGAACCGCTCTTCCGATC*T (IDT, HPLC, XXXXXXXX = 8nt index) This primer contains the full Illumina P7 sequence, followed by an index, followed by the Illumina PE read 2 sequence.
Cycle as follows: 95 °C 00:02:00 14 cycles of: 94 °C 00:01:20 65 °C 00:00:30 72 °C 00:00:30 Followed by: 72 °C 00:03:00 4 °C hold.
All subsequent steps are performed outside of pre-PCR room.

Make 2 pools of 48 libraries (5ul each) in 1.5ml tubes. The number of samples per pool depends on the number of indexes used. e.g. we typically use only 48 indexes and so make two pools of 48 samples each.
Prepare GpC methylase reaction buffer: %s %s
go to step #7
go to step #8
go to step #11
go to step #15
Place on magnet until beads pelette. Aspirate 15 µL and transfer to empty lobind plate for gDNA collection.

Place on a magnet until beads pellet. Remove supernatent, wash twice with 100 µL, 80% ethanol . Remove supernatent. Immediatley resuspend in 10 µL of nuclease free water. We use an Agilent Bravo for these wash steps.
Plate on magnet until beads pellet. Remove supernatent.
Add 180 µL, freshly prepared 80% ethanol . Mix thoroughly.
go to step #58
Place on magnet until beads pellet. Remove supernatent.
Add 100 µL, M-Desulphonation buffer (Zymo) . Mix thoroughly.
Incubate 00:15:00 Incubate for ≥ 15 minutes and ≤ 25 minutes
Place on magnet and remove supernatent.
Add 180 µL, freshly prepared 80% ethanol . Mix thoroughly.
Place on magnet until beads pellet. Remove supernatent.
Dry beads 60 °C 00:10:00
go to step #63
Elute cDNA in 25 µL, water

Check quality of a subet (e.g. 11 samples) of cDNA samples using a Bioanalyzer high sensitivty DNA chip. Fragment size range of 1kb to 5kb. Concentration of 100 to 500 pg/ul. If concentration is higher then dilute samples with water prior to Nextera XT library prep.
This section is performed outside of pre-PCR room. In a 96 well or a 384 well plate, add 2.25ul Tagmentation mastermix to each well: %s %s
In a thermocycler incubate as follows: 55 °C 00:05:00 , 4 °C, pause
Add 0.75 µL, NT buffer using multichannel pipette. Centrifuge then vortex to mix and centrifuge again.

Add 2.25 µL, NPM (from Nextera XT kit) Add 1.5 µL, pre-mixed and diluted i7 and i5 primers Using a multichannel pipette. Centrifuge and vortex to mix. We prepare pre-mixed indexing primers in 96w plates for this step. Using multichannel pipettes, transfer each of 8 i5 primers to 12 columns and each of 12 i7 primers to 8 rows, then dilute 5-fold with water.
Cycle as follows: 72 °C 00:05:00 95 °C 00:02:00 10 cycles of: 95 °C 65 °C 72 °C Followed by: 72 °C 00:05:00 4 °C hold.
Pool 1 µL of each indexed cDNA library. Purify using 0.7x AMPure XP beads with 2x 80% ethanol washes.
QC using Bioanalyzer.
Place on magnet until beads precipitate. Remove supernatent. Wash twice with 80% ethanol. Remove supernatent and air-dry beads for 5 minutes at room temperature.
Incubate on a thermocylcer as follows: 4 °C 00:05:00 Slow ramp from 4 °C to 37 °C at 15s per 1C 37 °C 00:30:00
For the fifth and final round, incubate for an additional 1 hour: 37 °C 01:00:00 4 °C hold.
Using a multi-channel pipette, add 0.75ul of cDNA sample (100-500pg/ul) to the mastermix. Vortex the plate and spin down.
Add 180 µL, 80% ethanol on the magnet.
Remove supernatent and air dry at 50 °C 00:05:00
Remove supernatent. Air dry 50 °C 00:05:00
Tranfer samples to fresh 96w PCR plate.
Purify each pool with 168 µL, AMPure XP beads (i.e. 0.7x ratio), 2x 80% ethanol washes and elute in 100 µL, water .
Purify each pool a second time 70 µL, AMPure XP beads (i.e. 0.7x ratio), 2x 80% ethanol washes and elute in 100 µL, water .

QC using a Bioanalyser high sensitivity chip. Pooled and purified scBS libraries should have a fragment length of 300-1000bp (average 450-500). Pools containing smaller fragments (especially <200bp) will result in poor alignment rates due to the presence of adapter concatemers. These should be subjected to an additional 0.7x AMPure XP purification.
creator: Stephen Clark
